response.justification: While this candidate demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals and meets the minimum educational requirement (Bachelor's degree with 15+ years of experience), they lack any demonstrated expertise in the core competencies required for a Principal Performance Architect role. The resume shows no background in AI/deep learning optimization, GPU architecture, SoC/IP modeling, or hardware-software co-design for accelerators—the foundational areas the role focuses on. This is fundamentally a full-stack application developer whose career has been in web, mobile, and infrastructure services, not hardware performance optimization.
strengths: Extensive software engineering experience with demonstrated progression to staff/principal levels across multiple companies
Proven performance optimization skills shown through low-latency system development with async I/O, caching, and real-time database work
Strong systems-thinking and architectural depth across diverse technical domains including hardware interaction (LabWindows/CVI, audio systems)
concerns: Zero demonstrated experience with AI/deep learning—no mention of neural networks, TensorFlow, PyTorch, or workload optimization for ML
No GPU architecture, CUDA, or accelerator optimization experience; career focused on web/application development rather than hardware performance
Missing core domain expertise: no SoC/IP modeling, HDL experience, or hardware-software co-design work—the role's primary focus areas

key_factors.factor: Absence of AI/GPU accelerator domain expertise
No SoC/IP modeling or hardware design background
